```python
# test_title_case.py

import pytest

# Replace `your_module` with the module where title_case is defined
from your_module import title_case


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input_value, expected",
    [
        ("hello world", "Hello World"),
        ("the quick brown fox", "The Quick Brown Fox"),
        ("python", "Python"),
        ("PYTHON", "Python"),
        ("pYtHoN", "Python"),
        ("hello WORLD", "Hello World"),
    ],
)
def test_title_case_happy_path(input_value, expected):
    assert title_case(input_value) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input_value, expected",
    [
        ("", ""),
        ("   ", ""),
        ("  hello   world  ", "Hello World"),
        ("\thello\tworld\n", "Hello World"),
        ("hello\nworld", "Hello World"),
    ],
)
def test_title_case_whitespace_edge_cases(input_value, expected):
    assert title_case(input_value) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input_value, expected",
    [
        ("hello-world", "Hello-world"),
        ("don't stop", "Don't Stop"),
        ("123 abc", "123 Abc"),
        ("abc123 def456", "Abc123 Def456"),
        ("éclair café", "Éclair Café"),
    ],
)
def test_title_case_special_word_cases(input_value, expected):
    assert title_case(input_value) == expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input_value",
    [
        None,
        123,
        ["hello", "world"],
    ],
)
def test_title_case_invalid_non_string_inputs_raise_attribute_error(input_value):
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        title_case(input_value)


def test_title_case_bytes_input_raises_type_error():
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        title_case(b"hello world")
```
